description_long: This object consists of a pair of antique flintlock pistols, each featuring a long, octagonal metal barrel and a curved wooden grip. The barrels are metallic, likely steel or iron, with visible patina and wear, and the grips are made of dark, polished wood. The pistols have prominent trigger guards and exposed flintlock mechanisms, including hammers and frizzens, which are typical of early firearm designs. The overall form is elegant and slightly ornate, with the wood and metal components showing signs of age and use. The trigger guards are rounded and substantial, providing protection for the trigger area. These pistols are compact and would have been used as personal sidearms, often seen in historical or period urban settings.
description: A pair of antique flintlock pistols with long metal barrels and curved wooden grips, featuring exposed firing mechanisms and rounded trigger guards. The surfaces show patina and wear, highlighting their historical character.
description_view_0: Side view showing both pistols' long barrels, curved wooden grips, and the exposed flintlock mechanisms.
description_view_1: Angled front view highlighting the open muzzles, rounded trigger guards, and the curvature of the grips.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the length of the barrels, the patina on the metal, and the full curvature of the wooden grips.
description_view_3: Angled rear view focusing on the back of the grips and the overall compactness of the pistols.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel (each pistol), no ribbing or venting visible, barrels are long and octagonal
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized trigger guard with a smooth, continuous curve and no finger grooves
